title: [Editorial] Samsung TV Plus and the New Era of TV Viewing
summary: For decades, TV has been the most familiar screen in daily life. From shared living room viewing in the analog era to today’s personalized, multi-device experience, TV has continually evolved — redefi

sources.title: [Editorial] Samsung TV Plus and the New Era of TV Viewing
sources.excerpt: For decades, TV has been the most familiar screen in daily life. From shared living room viewing in the analog era to today’s personalized, multi-device experience, TV has continually evolved — redefining how we watch. At the center of this shift is the connected TV market. Unlike traditional TV, where viewers follow fixed programming schedules, connected TVs allow users to discover and enjoy content in a more personalized way. By linking content, services and how people watch, they are driving

sources.title: Samsung Knox Now Protects Displays With Industry-Recognized CC Certification
sources.excerpt: Samsung Electronics today announced that its security solution, Samsung Knox, has achieved Common Criteria (CC) certification for monitors, expanding the verified security it has maintained on smart TVs for a decade. CC certification is a global standard used to assess the security integrity of products that need data protection, providing an international framework that consolidates various assessment criteria from different countries and facilitates mutual recognition. sources.title: [World Water Day] Samsung’s Promise of Water for All
sources.excerpt: Ahead of World Water Day on March 22, Samsung Electronics marked the 34th anniversary with initiatives to protect water resources. Designated by the United Nations in 1992, World Water Day raises awareness of global water scarcity and pollution. This year, Samsung’s offices and business sites worldwide led environmental efforts within and beyond the company — from supporting biodiversity and advancing water stewardship to providing emergency drinking water for vulnerable communities. sources.title: Samsung Addresses SME Cyber Risks With Galaxy S26 Ultra Enterprise Edition

The findings estimate that a single cyberattack could cost SMEs up to £100,000 in lost revenue and fines, underscoring the urgency of stronger security measures. sources.title: [World Sleep Day] How Samsung Is Tackling Sleep Apnea to Energize Your Days
sources.excerpt: Millions worldwide suffer from sleep apnea, but 80% of sufferers1 don’t even know they have it. This serious condition causes people to experience pauses in breathing while asleep — potentially disrupting oxygen supply, lowering sleep quality and leading to health complications including hypertension and stroke. Despite these risks, the disorder largely goes unnoticed because getting a diagnosis usually requires time-consuming and costly in-lab sleep studies. To mark World Sleep Day 2026, a glob

title: Microsoft and Nvidia launch AI partnership to speed up nuclear power plant permitting and construction — simulation tools and generative models could hasten historically lengthy processes
summary: Microsoft and Nvidia are joining forces to accelerate the construction of nuclear power plants for power-hungry AI data centers. The partnership combines generative AI, d

sources.title: Microsoft and Nvidia launch AI partnership to speed up nuclear power plant permitting and construction — simulation tools and generative models could hasten historically lengthy processes
sources.excerpt: Microsoft and Nvidia are joining forces to accelerate the construction of nuclear power plants for power-hungry AI data centers. The partnership combines generative AI, digital twin simulation, and Nvidia's Omniverse platform to streamline the nuclear lifecycle from permitting through operations.

title: AYANEO's upcoming Next 2 handheld gaming console shelved due to rising component prices — company stops preorders for the $1,999 Strix Halo device
summary: Ayaneo has suspended sales of its next-gen premium gaming handheld as the procurement costs for storage have slipped out of control. When it was first announced, prices w

sources.title: AYANEO's upcoming Next 2 handheld gaming console shelved due to rising component prices — company stops preorders for the $1,999 Strix Halo device
sources.excerpt: Ayaneo has suspended sales of its next-gen premium gaming handheld as the procurement costs for storage have slipped out of control. When it was first announced, prices were already high, but following the CNY break, vendor quotes had shot up many times, making it unfeasible to build the device. It's a temporary suspension, however, and existing sales will be honored.

title: Huawei unveils new Atlas 350 AI accelerator with 1.56 PFLOPS of FP4 compute and up to 112GB of HBM — claims 2.8x more performance than Nvidia's H20
summary: The Atlas 350 is using the Ascend 950PR chip, but it looks like a cut-down version with less compute. Translation overhead aside, previous reports and announcements have

sources.title: Huawei unveils new Atlas 350 AI accelerator with 1.56 PFLOPS of FP4 compute and up to 112GB of HBM — claims 2.8x more performance than Nvidia's H20
sources.excerpt: The Atlas 350 is using the Ascend 950PR chip, but it looks like a cut-down version with less compute. Translation overhead aside, previous reports and announcements have already revealed the full specs of this silicon, such as 128 GB of HBM and up to 2 PFLOPS of FP4 compute, but the Atlas 350 is being reported just a smidge below those numbers.

title: US senators want to suspend Nvidia AI chip export licenses to China and its intermediaries — bipartisan letter to Commerce Dept says that Huang’s claims of no chip diversion ‘were contradicted by reporting available’

sources.title: US senators want to suspend Nvidia AI chip export licenses to China and its intermediaries — bipartisan letter to Commerce Dept says that Huang’s claims of no chip diversion ‘were contradicted by reporting available’
sources.excerpt: U.S. senators Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) and Jim Banks (R-Ind.) told Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick that he should suspend all active export licenses to China for Nvidia AI chips, saying that Nvidia's most advanced AI GPUs are being diverted into the country despite Jensen Huang's assurances.

sources.title: FCC bans import of new consumer routers not made in the US over security threat — agency says foreign-made devices pose ‘unacceptable risk’ to US persons
sources.excerpt: The FCC says that it will no longer certify foreign-made routers, effectively making them illegal to sell in the U.S., unless the manufacturer can secure a "Conditional Approval" from the Department of War or the Department of Homeland Security.
